input.prompt: Extract the full README content, the repository description, topics/tags, number of stars, and any key information about this project. Return the complete README text.

toolCalls.result:
# HKUDS/Vibe-Trading — Repository Summary

**Stars:** 11.4k | **Forks:** 2.2k | **License:** MIT

**Description:** "Vibe-Trading: Your Personal Trading Agent"

**Tagline:** "One Command to Empower Your Agent with Comprehensive Trading Capabilities"

**Tech Stack:** Python 3.11+, FastAPI backend, React 19 frontend. Available via PyPI (`vibe-trading-ai`).

---

## Key README Content (Extracted)

### What It Is
A personal AI trading agent that combines LLM-driven research, multi-market backtesting, a quant alpha zoo (452 pre-built factors), multi-agent swarm coordination, and broker connectors — all accessible via CLI, Web UI, REST API, or MCP (Model Context Protocol).

### Key Features

- **Self-Improving Trading Agent** — natural-language market research, strategy drafting, memory-backed workflows
- **Multi-Agent Trading Teams** — investment committee, quant desk, risk committee, and crypto teams with streaming progress
- **Cross-Market Data & Backtesting** — A/HK/US equities, crypto, futures, forex with data-source fallback chains
- **Shadow Account** — extracts strategy rules from broker trade journals, backtests what you "left on the table"
- **Alpha Zoo** — 452 pre-built quant alphas across 4 zoos (qlib158, alpha101, gtja191, academic)
- **Broker Connectors** — 10 brokers including IBKR, Robinhood, Tiger, Alpaca, OKX, Binance, Futu, Dhan, Shoonya, Longbridge
- **Swarm Architecture** — DAG-based multi-agent runs with live status streaming
- **MCP Support** — 47+ MCP tools for integration with Claude and other MCP clients

### News Highlights (most recent)
- **2026-06-09:** Clearer error messaging for remote Web UI access without API_AUTH_KEY
- **2026-06-08:** Gemini 3.x multi-turn tool-calling fix for `thought_signature` round-tripping
- **2026-06-07:** Live swarm status cards in chat timeline; improved preset routing
- **2026-06-06:** Alpha compare tool for head-to-head alpha benchmarking across CLI/Web/REST/agent
- **2026-06-05:** Dhan + Shoonya connectors (India market); Gemini thinking model fixes; Chinese docstrings on all 452 Alpha Zoo factors

### Quick Start
One-command install via pip: `pip install vibe-trading-ai`

The project supports CLI interactive mode, Web UI, Docker deployment, and MCP server modes.

### Ecosystem
- **Website:** vibetrading.wiki
- **Docs:** vibetrading.wiki/docs
- **Community:** Feishu, WeChat, and Discord groups
- **Multi-language README:** English, 中文, 日本語, 한국어, العربية

### Architecture
The repo contains directories for agent logic, frontend (React), tools, scripts, wiki content, devcontainer config, and Docker deployment files. It includes CI/CD via GitHub Actions and a full test suite (197 frontend vitest tests plus backend security/auth tests).

---

*Note: The README content was partially truncated in the source, so the complete raw Markdown was not fully available, but all major sections above were extracted from what was present.*

toolCalls.name: WebFetch

input.prompt: Extract the complete README markdown content, including all sections about features, installation, usage, architecture, configuration, and any code examples. Return the full text.

toolCalls.result:
# Vibe-Trading README — Comprehensive Summary

## Overview

The README introduces Vibe-Trading as an open-source research workspace that "connects natural-language prompts to market-data loaders, strategy generation, backtest engines, reports, exports, and persistent research memory." It is hosted under the HKUDS GitHub organization and licensed under MIT.

The project supports 10+ languages via README translations (Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Arabic).

## News (excerpts on recent releases)

The most recent entries (2026-06-02 through 2026-06-09) cover: clearer error messages for remote Web UI access requiring an API key; fixes for Gemini 3.x multi-turn tool-calling where `thought_signature` was being dropped; live swarm status cards rendered in the chat timeline; a new `alpha compare` feature for head-to-head alpha benchmarking; two new Indian market connectors (Dhan and Shoonya); an opt-in local data cache for all seven data sources; and community triage improvements including trace correlation.

Earlier milestones include six new broker connectors (Tiger, Longbridge, Alpaca, OKX, Binance, Futu), v0.1.9 release with connector-first broker profiles, a connector-first broker architecture (IBKR + Robinhood), and the Alpha Zoo v1 (452 alphas across four zoos) shipping in v0.1.8.

## Key Features

Four core capabilities are highlighted:

1. **Self-Improving Trading Agent** — natural-language market research, strategy drafting, file/web analysis, memory-backed workflows
2. **Multi-Agent Trading Teams** — investment, quant, crypto, and risk committees with streaming progress and persisted reports
3. **Cross-Market Data & Backtesting** — A/HK/US equities, crypto, futures, forex; data fallback chains and composite backtests
4. **Shadow Account** — broker-journal behavior diagnostics, rule extraction, backtest comparisons, exportable audit reports

## Quick Start Options

Four installation paths are described:

| Path | Method |
|------|--------|
| **Docker** | Clone repo, copy `.env.example`, `docker compose up --build`, open port 8899 |
| **Local install** | `pip install -e .` from cloned repo, or `pip install vibe-trading-ai` from PyPI |
| **MCP plugin** | Use `vibe-trading-mcp` command for Claude Desktop, OpenClaw, Cursor, etc. |
| **ClawHub** | Single command: `npx clawhub@latest install vibe-trading --force` |

The package name on PyPI is `vibe-trading-ai`. Three CLI entry points are installed: `vibe-trading` (interactive TUI), `vibe-trading serve` (FastAPI web server), and `vibe-trading-mcp` (MCP server over stdio).

## Prerequisites

The README states an LLM API key from any supported provider is needed — or Ollama for local, key-free usage. Python 3.11+ is required for local install. Supported LLM providers include OpenRouter, OpenAI, DeepSeek, Gemini, Groq, DashScope/Qwen, Zhipu, Moonshot/Kimi, MiniMax, Xiaomi MIMO, Z.ai, and Ollama. The docs note that "all markets work without any API keys thanks to automatic fallback" via free sources like yfinance, OKX, mootdx, and AKShare.

## Environment Variables

Key configuration variables include:

- **LANGCHAIN_PROVIDER** — provider name (required)
- **Provider-specific API keys** (e.g., `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`)
- **LANGCHAIN_MODEL_NAME** — model selection
- **TUSHARE_TOKEN** — optional A-share data token
- **API_AUTH_KEY** — recommended for non-local deployments
- **VIBE_TRADING_ENABLE_SHELL_TOOLS** — opt-in for shell tools in remote deployments

### Recommended Models

Models are tiered: "Best" tier includes Claude Opus/Sonnet, GPT-5.5 Pro, Gemini 3.5 Flash for complex swarms; "Sweet spot" (default) includes DeepSeek v4 Pro, Grok 4.20, Kimi K2.6 for daily driving at ~1/10 the cost; small distilled variants are flagged as unreliable for tool calling.

## CLI Reference

The interactive TUI offers slash commands including `/help`, `/skills`, `/swarm`, `/swarm run`, `/list`, `/show`, `/code`, `/pine`, `/trace`, `/continue`, `/sessions`, `/settings`, `/clear`, and `/quit`. Single-run commands support `-p` for prompts, `--json` output, `--upload`, and `--swarm-run`. Alpha zoo commands include `alpha list`, `alpha show`, and `alpha bench`.

## Shadow Account Workflow

A five-step process: read broker journal → profile behavior (holding days, win rate, PnL ratio, drawdown, disposition effect, overtrading, momentum chasing, anchoring) → extract rules → run shadow backtest → deliver HTML/PDF report.

## Examples

Usage examples cover: moving average crossover on US equities, RSI mean-reversion on crypto, multi-factor A-share strategies, alpha zoo benchmarks, market research on equities and crypto, swarm workflows (investment committee, quant strategy desk, crypto trading desk), cross-session memory, and document upload/analysis.

## API Server

`vibe-trading serve --port 8899` exposes REST endpoints for runs, sessions (with SSE event streaming), swarm operations, alpha browsing/benchmarking, and settings management. Interactive docs are at `http://localhost:8899/docs`. Security defaults require `API_AUTH_KEY` for non-local clients and restrict shell-capable tools to localhost workflows.

## Web UI Settings

A Settings page allows updating LLM provider/model, base URL, generation parameters, and data source credentials. Settings persist to `agent/.env` and require `API_AUTH_KEY` for non-loopback access.

## MCP Plugin (36 Tools)

The MCP server exposes tools across research, trading, and analysis categories. Configuration examples are provided for Claude Desktop (`claude_desktop_config.json`), OpenClaw (`~/.openclaw/config.yaml`), and Cursor/Windsurf. Swarm workers can also call tools from external MCP servers via an operator-configured allowlist.

## Loading External MCP Tools (Client Mode)

This section covers the reverse direction — Vibe-Trading's agent calling tools from external MCP servers. A `~/.vibe-trading/agent.json` config file supports stdio, SSE, and streamable HTTP transports. An IBKR read-only probe is documented with OAuth flow, and local TWS/IB Gateway connections are supported via the `ibkr` optional dependency. Per-session MCP server overrides are supported via API session config.

## Project Structure

The repository is organized with an `agent/` backend (Python) containing CLI, API server, MCP server, agent core, memory, tools (31 auto-discovered), factors (452 alphas), skills (77), swarm engine (29 presets), session management, providers, and backtest engines (7 engines + composite + 7 data sources). The frontend uses React 19 + Vite + TypeScript with pages for Home, Agent, AlphaZoo, RunDetail, Compare, Correlation, and Settings.

## Ecosystem

The project belongs to the HKUDS agent ecosystem alongside NanoBot, AI-Trader, CLI-Anything, OpenSpace, and ClawTeam.

## Roadmap

| Phase | Status |
|-------|--------|
| Trust Layer (run cards, tool traces, citations) | Shipped |
| Hypothesis Registry | Backend MVP shipped |
| Research Autopilot | Next |
| Data Bridge (local CSV/Parquet/SQL) | Planned |
| Options Lab (vol surface, Greeks) | Planned |
| Portfolio Studio | Planned |
| Alpha Zoo (452 alphas) | Shipped 0.1.8 |
| Research Delivery (scheduled briefs) | Planned |
| Community (shareable skills) | Exploring |

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome via CONTRIBUTING.md. Good first issues are tagged. Contributors from the v0.1.9 cycle are credited, including @toanalien, @ai7eam-dev, @shadowinlife, @DoubleSky123, @ArthurXi, and others.

## Disclaimer

The README states Vibe-Trading "holds no funds" and "runs no execution venue." Broker trading is experimental, opt-in, bounded by user-set limits, and haltable at any time. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

## License

MIT License — full text in LICENSE file. The Alpha Zoo includes an Apache-2.0 attribution for Qlib alphas, with per-zoo LICENSE.md files for the formula-based zoos (Kakushadze 101, GTJA 191, academic factors).

toolCalls.name: WebFetch

input.prompt: Extract the PyPI package description, version, installation instructions, and any key details about vibe-trading-ai.

toolCalls.result:
# vibe-trading-ai — Summary

## Package Info
- **Version:** 0.1.9 (released June 1, 2026)
- **License:** MIT
- **Author:** HKUDS
- **Python Requirement:** >=3.11
- **Tags:** finance, trading, backtesting, agent, swarm, quantitative
- **Status:** Beta (Development Status 4)
- **Maintainer:** WARREN618

## Installation

```
pip install vibe-trading-ai
```

Three CLI entry points are provided: `vibe-trading` (interactive TUI), `vibe-trading serve` (FastAPI web server), and `vibe-trading-mcp` (MCP server for clients like Claude Desktop).

## What It Is

Vibe-Trading is an open-source research workspace that connects natural-language prompts to market-data loaders, backtest engines, report generation, and persistent research memory. It can be extended to autonomous trading through user-authorized brokers (e.g., Robinhood) with configurable limits and an instant kill switch.

The project is part of the HKUDS agent ecosystem and includes code from Microsoft Qlib (Apache-2.0), Kakushadze's 101 Formulaic Alphas (arXiv paper), and GTJA's 191 factors (formulas treated as mathematical content).

## Key Capabilities

- **77 finance skills** across 8 categories (data sourcing, strategy generation, analysis, risk, crypto, etc.)
- **29 swarm presets** for multi-agent trading teams (investment committee, crypto desk, risk committee, etc.)
- **Alpha Zoo** — 452 pre-built quant alphas across 4 zoos (qlib158, alpha101, gtja191, academic)
- **Cross-market data & backtesting** — A/HK/US equities, crypto, futures, forex with composite engines
- **Shadow Account** — broker journal profiling and rule-based strategy comparison
- **36 MCP tools** exposed for MCP-compatible clients
- **Cross-session persistent memory** and editable skills

## Data Sources

Free sources include AKShare (A-shares), yfinance (HK/US), OKX (crypto), CCXT (100+ exchanges), and **mootdx** (A-shares via TCP, no token needed). Tushare token is optional; mootdx is the preferred no-token A-share fallback.

## Notable v0.1.9 Highlights

The latest release (June 1, 2026) introduced connector-first broker profiles (IBKR local read-only + Robinhood Agentic Trading with OAuth), 36 MCP tools, a swarm pass with live reconciliation, and a `retry_run` command. It also includes the `mootdx` no-token A-share loader and a terminal UI refresh.

## Quick Start Example

```
vibe-trading init
vibe-trading run -p "Backtest a BTC-USDT 20/50 moving-average strategy for 2024 and summarize return and drawdown"
```

## Additional Install Paths

- **Docker:** `git clone` → `docker compose up --build`
- **Local:** `pip install -e .` from cloned repo
- **ClawHub:** `npx clawhub@latest install vibe-trading --force`

## Disclaimer

The project describes itself as "research and trading software" that is "not investment advice," holds no funds, and runs no execution venue. Broker-trading capability is labeled experimental and "use it at your own risk."

input.command: gh repo view HKUDS/Vibe-Trading --json readme 2>/dev/null | head -100 || echo "---fallback---"

call_00_22saLnxfoYanNCx9zFQ57201.result:
Here is a comprehensive research report on the **HKUDS/Vibe-Trading** repository.

---

## 1. Repository Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading |
| **Stars** | 11,438 (as of June 9, 2026) |
| **Forks** | 2,227 |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Primary Language** | Python |
| **Latest PyPI Version** | 0.1.9 (released June 1, 2026) |
| **Python Requirement** | 3.11+ |
| **Tags** | trading, quantitative-finance, algorithmic-trading, multi-agent, ai-agent, llm, backtesting, fintech, mcp, python |

---

## 2. What It Is

Vibe-Trading is an **open-source personal AI trading agent** that connects natural-language prompts to market-data loaders, strategy generation, backtest engines, report generation, and persistent research memory. It functions as a research workspace that can optionally be extended to autonomous trading through user-authorized brokers with configurable limits and an instant kill switch.

The project belongs to the **HKUDS agent ecosystem** (alongside NanoBot, AI-Trader, CLI-Anything, OpenSpace, and ClawTeam).

---

## 3. Key Features

**Self-Improving Trading Agent**
- Natural-language market research and strategy drafting
- File and web analysis
- Memory-backed workflows with cross-session persistent memory

**Multi-Agent Trading Teams (Swarm Architecture)**
- 29 swarm presets for multi-agent trading teams
- Includes Investment Committee, Quant Desk, Crypto Desk, Risk Committee
- DAG-based multi-agent runs with live streaming progress
- Persisted reports from each agent in the swarm

**Cross-Market Data and Backtesting**
- A/HK/US equities, crypto, futures, forex
- Data-source fallback chains (free sources preferred)
- Composite backtest engines (7 engines + composite)
- 7 data sources total

**Alpha Zoo**
- 452 pre-built quant alpha factors across 4 zoos:
  - Qlib158 (from Microsoft Qlib, Apache-2.0 licensed)
  - Alpha101 (Kakushadze's 101 Formulaic Alphas)
  - GTJA191 (191 factors)
  - Academic factors
- All 452 factors now have Chinese docstrings
- Includes `alpha list`, `alpha show`, `alpha bench`, and `alpha compare` commands

**Shadow Account**
- Reads broker trade journals
- Profiles trading behavior (holding days, win rate, PnL ratio, drawdown, disposition effect, overtrading, momentum chasing, anchoring)
- Extracts strategy rules from past trades
- Runs shadow backtests to compare what happened vs. what could have been
- Exports HTML/PDF audit reports

**Broker Connectors**
- 10 brokers supported: IBKR, Robinhood, Tiger, Alpaca, OKX, Binance, Futu, Dhan, Shoonya, Longbridge
- Connector-first broker architecture
- IBKR local read-only probe with OAuth
- Robinhood Agentic Trading with OAuth
- Dhan and Shoonya for Indian markets

**MCP (Model Context Protocol) Support**
- 36+ MCP tools exposed for integration with Claude Desktop, OpenClaw, Cursor, Windsurf
- Vibe-Trading's agent can also call tools from external MCP servers (client mode)
- Configurable via `~/.vibe-trading/agent.json`

**77 Finance Skills**
- Categorized across 8 areas: data sourcing, strategy generation, analysis, risk, crypto, etc.

**Cross-Session Persistent Memory**

---

## 4. Architecture

The repository has two main components:

- **Backend (Python):** CLI, FastAPI web server, MCP server, agent core, memory, tools (31 auto-discovered), factors (452 alphas), skills (77), swarm engine (29 presets), session management, LLM providers, and backtest engines (7 + composite + 7 data sources)
- **Frontend (React 19 + Vite + TypeScript):** Pages for Home, Agent, AlphaZoo, RunDetail, Compare, Correlation, and Settings

Data flows: Natural language prompt -> LLM -> tool selection -> market data loaders -> strategy generation -> backtest engine -> report -> persistent memory.

---

## 5. What Makes It Unique

1. **All-in-one platform:** Combines LLM-powered research, strategy generation, backtesting, alpha factor library, multi-agent swarms, and broker connectivity in a single codebase -- all accessible via CLI, Web UI, REST API, or MCP.

2. **Free data by default:** All markets work without any API keys using automatic fallback to free sources (yfinance, OKX, mootdx, AKShare). A-shares use `mootdx` via TCP with no token needed.

3. **Shadow Account concept:** Reverse-engineers trading behavior from broker journals and backtests alternative strategies -- a unique diagnostics feature.

4. **LLM-agnostic:** Supports 12+ LLM providers (OpenRouter, OpenAI, DeepSeek, Gemini, Groq, DashScope/Qwen, Zhipu, Moonshot/Kimi, MiniMax, Xiaomi MIMO, Z.ai, Ollama) and can run fully local via Ollama.

5. **MCP-native:** Deep MCP integration in both directions -- exposes tools to MCP clients and can consume tools from external MCP servers.

6. **Swarm orchestration:** Multi-agent DAG-based workflows with live status streaming -- not just single-agent trading bots.

7. **452 pre-built quant alphas:** Ships with a massive library of academically recognized alpha factors ready for use.

---

## 6. Installation and Usage

### Installation Methods

**Option 1: PyPI (recommended)**
```bash
pip install vibe-trading-ai
```

**Option 2: Docker**
```bash
git clone https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading.git
cd Vibe-Trading
cp .env.example .env
docker compose up --build
# Opens on port 8899
```

**Option 3: Local from source**
```bash
git clone https://github.com/HKUDS/Vibe-Trading.git
cd Vibe-Trading
pip install -e .
```

**Option 4: ClawHub**
```bash
npx clawhub@latest install vibe-trading --force
```

### CLI Entry Points
- `vibe-trading` -- Interactive TUI with slash commands
- `vibe-trading serve --port 8899` -- FastAPI web server with REST API
- `vibe-trading-mcp` -- MCP server over stdio

### Prerequisites
- Python 3.11+
- An LLM API key (any supported provider) -- or Ollama for local, key-free usage
- No market data API keys required for basic usage (free data fallbacks)

### Quick Start
```bash
vibe-trading init
vibe-trading run -p "Backtest a BTC-USDT 20/50 moving-average strategy for 2024 and summarize return and drawdown"
```

### MCP Configuration (for Claude Desktop)
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "vibe-trading": {
      "command": "vibe-trading-mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

---

## 7. Notable Technical Details

- **LLM Model Tiers:** The README provides explicit model recommendations -- "Best" tier (Claude Opus/Sonnet, GPT-5.5 Pro, Gemini 3.5 Flash), "Sweet spot" default (DeepSeek v4 Pro, Grok 4.20, Kimi K2.6 at ~1/10 the cost), with warnings that small distilled variants are unreliable for tool calling.

- **Security:** Shell-capable tools restricted to localhost by default. `API_AUTH_KEY` required for non-local Web UI access. Configurable via `VIBE_TRADING_ENABLE_SHELL_TOOLS`.

- **Test Suite:** 197 frontend vitest tests plus backend security/auth tests. CI/CD via GitHub Actions.

- **Dev Container:** Includes `.devcontainer` config for development environments.

- **Attribution:** Alpha Zoo includes Apache-2.0 licensed factors from Microsoft Qlib, formula-based factors from Kakushadze's 101 Formulaic Alphas (arXiv), and GTJA 191 factors (mathematical content).

- **Disclaimer:** The project explicitly states it holds no funds and runs no execution venue. Broker trading is experimental, opt-in, bounded by user-set limits, and haltable at any time.

- **Active Development:** Very active -- the changelog shows near-daily updates with significant feature additions (6 new brokers, alpha compare tool, swarm UI cards, Indian market connectors, and more in just the past month).

toolCalls.result:
## What the Provided Content Reveals

Based **solely** on the web page content you shared, I can tell you very little. The page contains:

1. **Site name / brand:** "Munger Mode" — likely a reference to Charlie Munger, the late vice-chairman of Berkshire Hathaway known for his investing philosophy and mental models.

2. **Technical details:**
   - Google Analytics 4 tracking (tag ID: `G-2MY4KG2STM`)
   - A dark-mode toggle script that sets the theme based on `localStorage`
   - The page defaults to dark mode unless the user has explicitly set light mode

3. **No substantive content** was captured — no headlines, body copy, feature lists, pricing, product descriptions, or calls to action.

## Honest Assessment

The content you provided is essentially just the `<head>` / analytics / theme boilerplate of a webpage. There is **insufficient information** to determine:

- What the website or product actually is
- Its key features
- Who it's for
- Any pricing or business model
- Any meaningful product details

## Recommendation

If you'd like a proper overview, I would need the **full rendered page content** — including visible body text, headings, navigation, and any product/landing page copy. With only the snippet above, any further claims would be pure speculation (e.g., guessing it's related to investing or mental models based on the name "Munger Mode"). I'm happy to provide a thorough analysis once you share the complete page content.

Here are the key findings from the web search about **Qiao Wang** in relation to **Messari**, product lead, and founder roles:

## Background
- **Qiao Wang** is a core contributor / founding partner at **Alliance DAO** (formerly DeFi Alliance), a crypto accelerator and investment fund.
- He joined **Messari** in **2017** as **Product Director** (later described as Founding Head of Product).
- He was a **quant trader** for about 10 years in traditional finance before entering crypto, trading equities and futures.

## Roles at Messari
- **Product Director / Founding Head of Product** — He was the early product leader at Messari, helping build its crypto asset information platform.
- He was active during Messari's early growth phase, often sharing market commentary on Twitter (e.g., about Bitcoin dominance, macro conditions, and crypto volatility).

## Education
- **Honours Bachelor of Mathematics** from the **University of Waterloo** (2010).

## Current Role
- **Founding Partner / Core Contributor** at **Alliance DAO** (DeFi Alliance), where he helps run an accelerator program for crypto startups, invests, and mentors founders.

## Key Takeaways
- **Not a founder of Messari** — That was Ryan Selkis. Qiao Wang was the early product lead (Product Director / Head of Product).
- He is deeply technical (math background, quant trading), and his move from traditional high-frequency trading to crypto was driven by interest in Bitcoin's volatility and upside potential.
- He is active on Twitter as **@qwqiao**.

The search results all point to **Munger Mode**, described as a long-term investment tool listed on Product Hunt. Here's a summary of what it does:

## Key Features

- **Tracking**: Monitors every SEC filing, earnings call, podcast, blog, insider trade, and superinvestor activity for the stocks you follow
- **Insight Extraction**: Automatically extracts the most important insights from all that data — so you don't have to read or watch everything yourself
- **Antithesis / Steelman**: For every insight, the tool also builds an **"antithesis"** to **steelman the opposite** view — meaning it actively constructs the best possible counterargument to challenge your thesis
- **Quarterly Deep Dive**: All insights culminate in a **comprehensive deep dive that updates quarterly**

## What It Is

It appears to be a platform/tool designed for fundamental, long-term investors — named after Charlie Munger — that synthesizes vast amounts of publicly available information into concise, actionable insights while deliberately forcing you to consider the opposing viewpoint (the "steelman" approach). This is a form of **red teaming** for investment theses.

## Where to Find It

The tool is listed on **Product Hunt** under the name "Munger Mode" — you can find more details, user reviews, and sign-up information there.

Here's what I found from the web search:

**Munger Mode** is a long-term investment tool listed on **Product Hunt**. According to its description, it:

- Tracks every **SEC filing**, earnings call, podcast, blog, insider trade, and "superinvestor" activity for stocks you follow.
- Extracts the most important insights from those sources.
- Builds an **"antithesis"** for every insight in order to **steelman the opposite** perspective — essentially creating a strong counterargument to test your thesis.
- Culminates in a quarterly deep dive report for each stock.

This directly connects the two concepts: "Munger mode" (a reference to Charlie Munger's disciplined, inversion-based thinking) with "steelmanning" (the practice of constructing the strongest possible version of an opposing argument). The tool applies both to the analysis of SEC filings and other financial data.

You can view the full listing here: [Munger Mode on Product Hunt](https://www.producthunt.com/products/munger-mode)

call_01_6aZ4aVXLqNdjyxAd99xH1688.result:
Here is a comprehensive research summary of **MungerMode**.

---

## 1. What It Does

MungerMode is a **long-term investment research tool** that aggregates public information about stocks you follow and distills it into concise, actionable insights. It monitors a wide range of sources and uses AI to extract the key takeaways, saving investors from having to read or watch everything themselves. Crucially, for every insight it surfaces, it also generates an **"antithesis"** -- a steelman version of the opposing argument, meant to challenge your thesis. All of this feeds into a **quarterly-updated deep dive** for each tracked stock.

The tool is named after **Charlie Munger**, the late vice-chairman of Berkshire Hathaway, who was famous for using mental models, inversion thinking, and aggressively challenging his own assumptions.

## 2. Key Features

- **Multi-source monitoring**: Tracks every SEC filing, earnings call, podcast, blog post, insider trade, and superinvestor portfolio activity for stocks you follow.
- **AI-powered insight extraction**: Extracts the most important insights from all that content so you do not have to consume it in full.
- **Antithesis/Steelman generation**: For each insight, the tool constructs a strong counterargument (the "antithesis") to force consideration of the opposing viewpoint -- a form of red-teaming for investment theses.
- **Quarterly deep dives**: All accumulated insights feed into a comprehensive deep-dive report that is updated each quarter for every tracked stock.

## 3. Who It Is For

Long-term fundamental investors who want to stay on top of a curated set of stocks without spending hours reading full SEC filings, listening to earnings calls, or tracking superinvestor moves. The steelman feature is particularly suited for investors who want to rigorously stress-test their own convictions -- a hallmark of Charlie Munger's approach to decision-making.

## 4. Technical & Other Details

| Detail | Information |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://mungermode.com |
| **Creator** | Qiao Wang (@qwqiao) |
| **Creator's background** | Founding Partner at Alliance DAO, former Product Lead / Founding Head of Product at Messari, former quant trader (~10 years in traditional finance), Honours BMath from University of Waterloo |
| **Launch date** | May 30, 2026 (very recently -- ~10 days ago) |
| **Product Hunt listing** | Listed under Fintech, Investing, and AI categories |
| **Pricing** | Free (no paid tiers found) |
| **Product Hunt score** | 4 (launch day), 0 reviews/comments so far |
| **Analytics** | Google Analytics 4 (G-2MY4KG2STM) |
| **Theme** | Dark mode by default with localStorage-based theme toggle |
| **Site status** | The public-facing site at mungermode.com appears to be a minimal shell (branding + analytics) -- the actual tool likely loads dynamically (JS-rendered) or requires login after signup |
| **Social presence** | No Twitter, GitHub, or other social links found for the product itself |

**No external articles, reviews, or third-party coverage** of MungerMode were found on the web. Given its very recent launch (May 30, 2026), it currently has almost no public footprint beyond the Product Hunt listing. The Product Hunt listing has zero reviews and zero comments.

---

- [MungerMode on Product Hunt](https://www.producthunt.com/products/munger-mode)
- [MungerMode Product Hunt Forum](https://www.producthunt.com/p/munger-mode)
- [MungerMode Reviews on Product Hunt](https://www.producthunt.com/products/munger-mode/reviews)
